Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Shishinokami |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |Deity = Shishinokami |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Shishinokami |Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Shishinokami |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |Deity = Shishinokami |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Shishinokami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == 概要 == 本節では2社共通事項について記述する。 祭神の四至神は神域（宮域）の[[守護神]]である伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ。[[四至]]、すなわち神域の四方の境界を守護する[[神 (神道)|神]]である櫻井（1989）：82ページ。 神社としての四至神は、四至神に対して祭儀を奉る[[祭壇]]である。[[社殿]]を持たない神社であり、[[磐座]]（いわくら）祭祀の形態を残している[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php 四至神|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=2009年6月25日}}"（2012年8月22日閲覧。）。 == 外宮の四至神== 外宮所管社の四至神は、外宮神域の九丈殿と五丈殿の建つ大庭（おおば）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：36ページ。九丈殿の南に一段高い石段があり、その上に[[サカキ|榊]]（さかき）が1本だけ立つという簡素なたたずまいである。榊の根元には特徴的な形をした石が据えられているため、参拝者の中には石を拝むものと勘違いする者がいるが、これは誤りである櫻井（1989）：83ページ。 『延暦儀式帳』に宮廻神（みやのめぐりのかみ）200余座を年に3度祭る旨が記されているのが最古の記録であるが、祭祀をどこで執り行ったのかは分かっていない。[[中世]]には複数の[[古文書]]に四至の神が44座あり、宮中で祭ると記されているが、やはり「宮中」の具体的な場所は不明である。[[近世]]になると「廻神16座」と称し、16所の石段の前で2月と10月の最初の午の日に榊や[[幣|御幣]]を立てて祭り、[[大晦日]]にも花榊を立て、供物を奉ったと記録されている。しかしながら、外宮境内には[[摂末社]]の[[遥拝]]所として石積みが多数築かれるようになり、四至神の鎮座地の石積みなのか、遥拝所の石積みなのか区別が付かなくなってしまった。 区別ができなくなったことから、[[明治]]の最初期に境内のすべての石積みの整理が行われた。しかし、由緒ある四至神の祭祀を絶やすわけにはいかないことから、[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に九丈殿で祭儀を行い、16所の四至神のうちの1所であった九丈殿前の石段を外宮所管社・四至神の祭場として定めた。以降は年に5回、[[奉幣]]の儀が四至神に捧げられる。 == 内宮の四至神 == 内宮所管社の四至神は、内宮神域の五丈殿東方に鎮座する。小さな石段が2段あり、その石段上に石神として祀られている。 内宮所管社の四至神も外宮所管社の四至神と同様の経緯をたどり、1所に祀られるようになった。 == 交通 == ;外宮の四至神 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 ;内宮の四至神 * JR参宮線・近鉄[[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2012年8月30日閲覧。）。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * 伊勢自動車道伊勢西ICより三重県道32号伊勢磯部線（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に駐車場あり。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:The four supreme deities]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

{{Shrine |Name = Shishinokami |Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Shishinokami |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |Deity = Shishinokami |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Shishinokami |Religious Affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Shishinokami |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |Deity = Shishinokami |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Shishinokami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == 概要 == 本節では2社共通事項について記述する。 祭神の四至神は神域（宮域）の[[守護神]]である伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ。[[四至]]、すなわち神域の四方の境界を守護する[[神 (神道)|神]]である櫻井（1989）：82ページ。 神社としての四至神は、四至神に対して祭儀を奉る[[祭壇]]である。[[社殿]]を持たない神社であり、[[磐座]]（いわくら）祭祀の形態を残している[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php 四至神|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=2009年6月25日}}"（2012年8月22日閲覧。）。 == 外宮の四至神== 外宮所管社の四至神は、外宮神域の九丈殿と五丈殿の建つ大庭（おおば）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：36ページ。九丈殿の南に一段高い石段があり、その上に[[サカキ|榊]]（さかき）が1本だけ立つという簡素なたたずまいである。榊の根元には特徴的な形をした石が据えられているため、参拝者の中には石を拝むものと勘違いする者がいるが、これは誤りである櫻井（1989）：83ページ。 『延暦儀式帳』に宮廻神（みやのめぐりのかみ）200余座を年に3度祭る旨が記されているのが最古の記録であるが、祭祀をどこで執り行ったのかは分かっていない。[[中世]]には複数の[[古文書]]に四至の神が44座あり、宮中で祭ると記されているが、やはり「宮中」の具体的な場所は不明である。[[近世]]になると「廻神16座」と称し、16所の石段の前で2月と10月の最初の午の日に榊や[[幣|御幣]]を立てて祭り、[[大晦日]]にも花榊を立て、供物を奉ったと記録されている。しかしながら、外宮境内には[[摂末社]]の[[遥拝]]所として石積みが多数築かれるようになり、四至神の鎮座地の石積みなのか、遥拝所の石積みなのか区別が付かなくなってしまった。 区別ができなくなったことから、[[明治]]の最初期に境内のすべての石積みの整理が行われた。しかし、由緒ある四至神の祭祀を絶やすわけにはいかないことから、[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に九丈殿で祭儀を行い、16所の四至神のうちの1所であった九丈殿前の石段を外宮所管社・四至神の祭場として定めた。以降は年に5回、[[奉幣]]の儀が四至神に捧げられる。 == 内宮の四至神 == 内宮所管社の四至神は、内宮神域の五丈殿東方に鎮座する。小さな石段が2段あり、その石段上に石神として祀られている。 内宮所管社の四至神も外宮所管社の四至神と同様の経緯をたどり、1所に祀られるようになった。 == 交通 == ;外宮の四至神 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 ;内宮の四至神 * JR参宮線・近鉄[[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2012年8月30日閲覧。）。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * 伊勢自動車道伊勢西ICより三重県道32号伊勢磯部線（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に駐車場あり。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |Deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == 概要 == 本節では2社共通事項について記述する。 祭神の四至神は神域（宮域）の[[守護神]]である伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ。[[四至]]、すなわち神域の四方の境界を守護する[[神 (神道)|神]]である櫻井（1989）：82ページ。 神社としての四至神は、四至神に対して祭儀を奉る[[祭壇]]である。[[社殿]]を持たない神社であり、[[磐座]]（いわくら）祭祀の形態を残している[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php 四至神|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=2009年6月25日}}"（2012年8月22日閲覧。）。 == 外宮の四至神== 外宮所管社の四至神は、外宮神域の九丈殿と五丈殿の建つ大庭（おおば）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：36ページ。九丈殿の南に一段高い石段があり、その上に[[サカキ|榊]]（さかき）が1本だけ立つという簡素なたたずまいである。榊の根元には特徴的な形をした石が据えられているため、参拝者の中には石を拝むものと勘違いする者がいるが、これは誤りである櫻井（1989）：83ページ。 『延暦儀式帳』に宮廻神（みやのめぐりのかみ）200余座を年に3度祭る旨が記されているのが最古の記録であるが、祭祀をどこで執り行ったのかは分かっていない。[[中世]]には複数の[[古文書]]に四至の神が44座あり、宮中で祭ると記されているが、やはり「宮中」の具体的な場所は不明である。[[近世]]になると「廻神16座」と称し、16所の石段の前で2月と10月の最初の午の日に榊や[[幣|御幣]]を立てて祭り、[[大晦日]]にも花榊を立て、供物を奉ったと記録されている。しかしながら、外宮境内には[[摂末社]]の[[遥拝]]所として石積みが多数築かれるようになり、四至神の鎮座地の石積みなのか、遥拝所の石積みなのか区別が付かなくなってしまった。 区別ができなくなったことから、[[明治]]の最初期に境内のすべての石積みの整理が行われた。しかし、由緒ある四至神の祭祀を絶やすわけにはいかないことから、[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に九丈殿で祭儀を行い、16所の四至神のうちの1所であった九丈殿前の石段を外宮所管社・四至神の祭場として定めた。以降は年に5回、[[奉幣]]の儀が四至神に捧げられる。 == 内宮の四至神 == 内宮所管社の四至神は、内宮神域の五丈殿東方に鎮座する。小さな石段が2段あり、その石段上に石神として祀られている。 内宮所管社の四至神も外宮所管社の四至神と同様の経緯をたどり、1所に祀られるようになった。 == 交通 == ;外宮の四至神 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 ;内宮の四至神 * JR参宮線・近鉄[[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == 概要 == 本節では2社共通事項について記述する。 祭神の四至神は神域（宮域）の[[守護神]]である伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ。[[四至]]、すなわち神域の四方の境界を守護する[[神 (神道)|神]]である櫻井（1989）：82ページ。 神社としての四至神は、四至神に対して祭儀を奉る[[祭壇]]である。[[社殿]]を持たない神社であり、[[磐座]]（いわくら）祭祀の形態を残している[[アイティービー|iTV]]"[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php 四至神|伊勢神宮百科辞典|伊勢志摩の旅よいとこせ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=2009年6月25日}}"（2012年8月22日閲覧。）。 == 外宮の四至神== 外宮所管社の四至神は、外宮神域の九丈殿と五丈殿の建つ大庭（おおば）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：36ページ。九丈殿の南に一段高い石段があり、その上に[[サカキ|榊]]（さかき）が1本だけ立つという簡素なたたずまいである。榊の根元には特徴的な形をした石が据えられているため、参拝者の中には石を拝むものと勘違いする者がいるが、これは誤りである櫻井（1989）：83ページ。 『延暦儀式帳』に宮廻神（みやのめぐりのかみ）200余座を年に3度祭る旨が記されているのが最古の記録であるが、祭祀をどこで執り行ったのかは分かっていない。[[中世]]には複数の[[古文書]]に四至の神が44座あり、宮中で祭ると記されているが、やはり「宮中」の具体的な場所は不明である。[[近世]]になると「廻神16座」と称し、16所の石段の前で2月と10月の最初の午の日に榊や[[幣|御幣]]を立てて祭り、[[大晦日]]にも花榊を立て、供物を奉ったと記録されている。しかしながら、外宮境内には[[摂末社]]の[[遥拝]]所として石積みが多数築かれるようになり、四至神の鎮座地の石積みなのか、遥拝所の石積みなのか区別が付かなくなってしまった。 区別ができなくなったことから、[[明治]]の最初期に境内のすべての石積みの整理が行われた。しかし、由緒ある四至神の祭祀を絶やすわけにはいかないことから、[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）に九丈殿で祭儀を行い、16所の四至神のうちの1所であった九丈殿前の石段を外宮所管社・四至神の祭場として定めた。以降は年に5回、[[奉幣]]の儀が四至神に捧げられる。 == 内宮の四至神 == 内宮所管社の四至神は、内宮神域の五丈殿東方に鎮座する。小さな石段が2段あり、その石段上に石神として祀られている。 内宮所管社の四至神も外宮所管社の四至神と同様の経緯をたどり、1所に祀られるようになった。 == 交通 == ;外宮の四至神 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩5分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩5分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停よりすぐ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]"（2011年1月8日閲覧。）。 ;内宮の四至神 * JR参宮線・近鉄[[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2012年8月30日閲覧。）。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * 伊勢自動車道伊勢西ICより三重県道32号伊勢磯部線（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に駐車場あり。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Shishijin) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Shishijin are {{ill|deities (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Shishijin serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Shishijin. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Shishijin | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Shishijin of the Outer Shrine == The Shishijin managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Shishijin, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Shishijin and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Shishijin rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Shishijin sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Shishijin. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Shishijin. == Shishijin of the Inner Shrine == The Shishijin managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Shishijin of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Shishijin of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Shishijin of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deities (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "皇大神宮}}" to "皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]
