Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-qq]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:qq-Miyanomeguri-no-Kami-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:New]]

The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:New]]

Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]] [[ja:四至神]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:守護神]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shishi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyanomeguri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} {{Shrine |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|deity (Shinto)|ja|神 (神道)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura|ja|磐座}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|sakaki|ja|サカキ}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|early modern period|ja|近世}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|gohei|ja|御幣}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote worship sites for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4年}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kōtsū|ja|三重交通}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|宇治山田駅}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p.
