[[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 18) |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=sakaki}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|WD=Q1011372|lt=gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=sakaki}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|WD=Q1011372|lt=gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)|1=Kami}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|WD=Q1011372|lt=gohei|1=Gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)|1=Kami}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|WD=Q1011372|lt=gohei|1=Gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] [[ja:四至神]][[zh:四至神]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)|1=Kami}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|WD=Q1011372|lt=gohei|1=Gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]

It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|qid=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四至神]] [[zh:%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[zh:四至神]] [[zh:四至神]] {{wikidata link|Q6543779}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Naiku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) |location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Geku Miyanomegurinokami.jpg|250px]]
Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine - Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}} |deity = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the Enryaku era |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Miyanomeguri-no-Kami |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 18) }} '''Miyanomeguri-no-Kami''' (四至神, *Miyanomeguri-no-Kami*) is both the name of a [[Shinto]] shrine and its enshrined deity located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. There are two shrines dedicated to this deity within the city: one is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine), and the other is under {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine). The deity '''四至神''' (''Miyanomeguri no Kami''), worshiped at Ise Jingu, is not directly associated with the number '''4''' as a sacred number. The '''"四至"''' in its name literally means '''"four boundaries"''' or '''"four directions"''', referring to the protective role of this deity over the '''four corners''' or '''boundaries''' of the sacred area (神域, ''shingi''). The number '''4''' here is descriptive, indicating the deity's function as a '''guardian of the perimeter''' of the shrine grounds, rather than symbolizing any numerological or spiritual importance. This is similar to other boundary-protecting deities in Shinto, where the focus is on spatial protection rather than numerology. == Overview == This section describes common matters related to both shrines. The guardian deity (Miyanomeguri-no-Kami) of the enshrined kami serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the sacred precincts (Miyauchi)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.115. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami are {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|qid=Q524158|lt=deity (Shinto)|1=Kami}} that protect the four boundaries of the sacred areaSakurai (1989): p.82. As a shrine, the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami serves as an altar dedicated to the rituals of the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. It is a shrine without a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}, preserving the style of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|qid=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}} worship[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php Miyanomeguri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090625151247/http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/m0003.php|date=June 25, 2009}} (Accessed August 22, 2012). == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Outer Shrine is enshrined in the Ōba (Great Garden), where the {{ill|Kujō-den|ja|九丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Gojō-den|ja|五丈殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are locatedIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.36. To the south of Kujō-den, there is a slightly elevated stone stairway, with a single {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|qid=Q775470|lt=sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}} tree standing simply on top. A distinctively shaped stone is placed at the base of the sakaki tree, which some worshippers mistakenly venerate, but this is incorrectSakurai (1989): p.83. The oldest record is found in the "Enryaku Gishikichō," mentioning over 200 shrines dedicated to Miyano Meguri no Kami, worshipped three times a year, though the specific ritual locations remain unknown. In the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, several {{ill|ancient documents|ja|古文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient documents|1=komonjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q89298567}} refer to 44 shrines of Miyanomeguri-no-Kami, said to be worshipped within the palace, though the exact site remains unclear. By the {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, these were called "Meguri no Kami 16 shrines," with rituals performed in front of stone steps in February and October, offering sakaki and sacred wands ({{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|qid=Q1011372|lt=gohei|1=Gohei}}), along with floral sakaki and offerings on New Year's Eve. However, as numerous stone arrangements were built within the Outer Shrine grounds as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for associated shrines, it became difficult to distinguish between stones for the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami and remote worship stones. Due to this confusion, all stone arrangements within the precincts were reorganized in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. To continue the venerable Miyanomeguri-no-Kami rituals, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}} (1871), ceremonies began at Kujō-den, and the stone steps in front of Kujō-den, previously one of the 16 Miyanomeguri-no-Kami sites, were designated as the ritual site for the Outer Shrine’s Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. Since then, offerings are made five times a year to the Miyanomeguri-no-Kami. == Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine == The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami managed by the Inner Shrine is enshrined to the east of the Gojō-den within the Inner Shrine precincts. It features two small stone steps, with the deity worshipped as a stone god atop them. The Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine followed a similar history to that of the Outer Shrine, being consolidated into a single site for worship. == Access == ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Outer Shrine * About a 5-minute walk from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} and {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|qid=Q900630|lt=Kintetsu Yamada Line|1=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}}, via the {{ill|Gekū Sando|ja|外宮参道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekū Sando|qq=}}. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise-shi Eki-mae" bus stop on the {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kōtsū|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus line. * Immediately accessible from the "Gekū-mae" bus stop on Mie Kōtsū. * About a 5-minute drive north from the {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32 Ise-Isobe Line|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). Parking available nearby[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information] (Accessed January 8, 2011). ; Miyanomeguri-no-Kami of the Inner Shrine * About a 15-minute ride on a Mie Kōtsū bus from the south exit (JR side) of Ise Station on the JR Sangū Line or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at Naikū-maeMie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Information - Visit Mie]" (Accessed August 30, 2012). * Also accessible from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line via Mie Kōtsū bus to Naikū-mae. * About a 5-minute drive south from Ise-nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road) or from the {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} via National Route 23, with parking available nearby. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 == See Also == * [[日本の神の一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0108.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 皇大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0208.html 四至神（みやのめぐりのかみ） 豊受大神宮所管社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|四至神|version=97033287|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q6543779|ja|四至神|zh|四至神|zh|%E5%9B%9B%E8%87%B3%E7%A5%9E}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tutelary deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
